title: A Poincare-Birkhoff theorem for C0-Hamiltonian maps
speaker: Agustin Moreno
tags: Special Joint IAS/PU Symplectic Geometry Seminar

proof_stages.label: Spatial restricted three-body Hamiltonian and low-energy regions
proof_stages.summary: The talk formulates the circular restricted three-body problem, identifies the mass and energy parameters, describes Lagrange critical values, and isolates the low-energy near-primary components with collision singularities.

proof_stages.label: Moser regularization and contact compactification
proof_stages.summary: Binary collisions are compactified by a Moser-type transformation, producing compact regularized energy hypersurfaces such as S^3 x S^2 that carry standard contact structures whose Reeb dynamics reparametrizes the original flow, with the planar problem as an invariant contact submanifold.

proof_stages.label: Open-book global sections and Hamiltonian return maps
proof_stages.summary: Poincare's planar global-section strategy is recast in higher dimensions via open books adapted to the Reeb flow, yielding for the spatial problem an explicit adapted open book with binding the planar problem and page modeled on a cotangent domain of S^2, so that the flow reduces to a Hamiltonian return map.

proof_stages.label: C0 boundary trade-off and infinite twist condition
proof_stages.summary: Because one can make either the boundary symplectic geometry smooth or the boundary dynamics smooth but not both, the speaker fixes the symplectic setup and defines C0-Hamiltonian twist maps: Hamiltonian in the interior, only continuous at the boundary, and with contact-direction twisting that blows up at the boundary.

proof_stages.label: Poincare-Birkhoff theorem for C0-Hamiltonian twist maps
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem states that, under isolated fixed-point, c1=0, and nonvanishing-in-infinitely-many-degrees symplectic homology assumptions, such a twist map has simple interior periodic points of arbitrarily large minimal period; for cotangent-type domains these homological assumptions are automatic.

proof_stages.label: Smoothing C0 maps and passing to admissible Hamiltonians
proof_stages.summary: The proof first approximates the C0 infinite-twist Hamiltonian by C1 Hamiltonians with finite but increasing twist, then extends them to the completion as admissible Hamiltonians whose slopes tend to infinity so their Floer homology limits compute symplectic homology.

proof_stages.label: Action filtration excluding boundary generators
proof_stages.summary: A quantitative twist estimate forces the Hamiltonian action of boundary and artificially added end orbits to escape to negative infinity, so in the limiting symplectic homology computation only interior periodic orbits can contribute.

proof_stages.label: Prime-iteration Floer-degree contradiction
proof_stages.summary: Assuming finitely many periodic points, one takes large prime iterates to avoid existing periods and uses degree support, Conley-Zehnder indices, and local Floer homology bounds for degenerate orbits to contradict the infinitely many nonzero symplectic homology degrees.

examples.name: Near-primary Hill-region spacecraft scenarios
examples.note: Satellites near Earth, halo-orbit missions near the Moon, and benign distant asteroids are used to make the three connected low-energy components and Hill stability physically concrete.
examples.engagement_reason: He writes the restricted three-body Hamiltonian, analyzes low-energy near-primary Hill components and their topology, and later regularizes these energy levels for the dynamics.

examples.name: Gravity-assist perturbations of collision orbits
examples.note: The speaker explains, after an audience slingshot remark, that regularized collision orbits model limiting trajectories that can be perturbed to near-collision gravity assists.
examples.engagement_reason: He uses collision orbits as the object of Moser regularization, describing the coordinate swap, compactification at infinity, collision sphere, and resulting compact energy level.

passes.reason: The collision-orbit slingshot limit is a singular limiting trajectory whose nondegenerate perturbations model gravity assists.

examples.name: Cotangent-sphere Liouville domains
examples.note: Fiberwise star-shaped domains in T*S^n, especially the T*S^2 case relevant to the spatial three-body return map, are given as a concrete class satisfying the theorem's homological hypotheses.
examples.engagement_reason: He applies the theorem to fiberwise star-shaped domains in T*S^n, checking the C1 and symplectic homology conditions and identifying the n=2 three-body case.

passes.reason: Fiberwise star-shaped domains in cotangent bundles of spheres are standard members of an infinite cotangent-domain family satisfying the hypotheses.
